mechanism_narrative: A rising Index of Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output, which signals economic strength and supports higher corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines month-over-month but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, or if rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals weakening labor market conditions and eroding consumer demand, which depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 short-term returns.
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition is met, the average 5-day forward return is not negative at a 95% confidence level, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in both the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation tightens financial conditions and reduces real money supply, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint condition leads to positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the interaction term in a linear regression (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) is not statistically significant at the 10% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) during the post-recession recovery phase signals robust consumer spending that drives GDP growth and corporate earnings, lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If during the post-recession recovery regime, the trigger condition does not result in positive 60-day returns in at least 70% of instances, or if the average return is not significantly different from zero at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect a healthy labor market that supports consumption and economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If monthly PAYEMS changes are negative but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such cases, or if the rank IC over any 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in year-over-year CPI inflation above 3% erodes real purchasing power and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which pressures equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns in the near term.
failure_condition: If after the trigger, the average 20-day forward return is not negative at a 90% confidence level, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand and supply strength, reinforcing economic momentum and boosting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% and INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce positive 60-day returns in at least 65% of cases over a 10-year period, or if the interaction term in a regression (HOUST_growth * INDPRO_growth) is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate dropping below 4.5% signals an extremely tight labor market, which fuels wage inflation and forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance, leading to negative S&P 500 returns in the very short term.
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition, the average 5-day forward return is not negative at a 95% confidence level, or if the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over a 3-year rolling window.
